IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Thursday, July 9, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is the heaviest board out there right now — 36 calls, 189 hands sitting, and the work is *real*. Ermco's Google Data Center in Mooresville is running 5-10s with Saturday 8s, straight new construction outside work. Fluor's Eli Lilly play in Lebanon pays **$58.70/hr** on second shift (5-10s, possible Sat), and Meade's Meta facility hits **$53.80/hr** but throws 10% scale bumps on top of it — that's a **$59.18/hr** effective rate for 5-12s Mon-Fri plus Saturday 10s. This board has depth.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is locked in on Microsoft Mount Pleasant — four contractors pulling from the same job, all posted at **$62.51/hr**, and they're running long sleeves all day. Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, and Kelso Burnett are all hammering the same site. You need reciprocal Wisconsin credentials or two years verifiable, but if you got it, this is solid work.

**LU-26 Lanham, MD** is data center heavy: FIS, Dynalectric, and JE Richards all posting Virginia Sterling and Ashburn facilities around **$48.92/hr** base, **$125/day per diem**, planned OT. That Ladbrook Drive FIS job is active with customer background required — largest call at 45 hands.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** is thin on volume (9 calls, 104 hands deep) but pay stacks fast. Summit Line and Rosendin both hitting **$55/hr** base with **$15/hr incentives** for 90% of 60-hour weeks. You need NM State JIW License EE98J and OSHA 10.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has tight bandwidth (9 calls only) but Lake Mariner Data and Amazon are both offering **$48.20/hr** with per diem available. O'Connell and Ferguson orientations run Mon/Tue/Thu on Amazon and Lake Mariner—first shift, 5-10s and 1-8 Mon-Fri.

The play right now: data centers are vacuuming hands faster than anything else on the board. If you can swing the background checks and drug screens, your rate locked in within two weeks.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Thursday, July 9, 2026**

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** — 47 calls on the board but only 61 hands, so you're looking at real scarcity up north. Golden Valley Electric Association is hiring laborers at **$30.86/hr** for inventory work at Healy Powerhouse, which is grunt money, but New Horizons Telecom has cell tower tech gigs across the state at **$24.18–$55.06/hr** depending on assignment. Wolf Creek Federal Services is running a **$55.84/hr** fire alarm play at Ft. Greely if you've got DoD Secret clearance.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — 27 calls, 94 hands, and the Polaris Forge Data Center job in Harwood is the heavy lifter: Parsons Electric is posting 28 inside JW at **$44.80/hr** plus **$100/day PD** (or **$200/day total if you grab the ND license bonus**) working 6-10s. Hunt Electric's Air Base work is softer at the same rate but cleaner 4-10 schedule. Book's getting pushed, so move quick.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** — 9 calls, 104 hands; this one's packed. Summit Line is burning 40 inside JW at **$55/hr flat**, all OT double time, NM state license required. Rosendin's got 37 more at **$40/hr** plus **$15 incentive** (hitting 90% of 60 hrs/wk), also double time OT. Both are project-length data center work.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** — Substation tech plays dominating: MJ Electric's Greenfield 345kV in New Carlisle pays **$56.66/hr** plus **$100/day PD**; Hydro-Exc. runs **$56.66** with **$125/day** on the NIPSCO side. 27 calls, 38 hands.

**LU-125 Portland, OR** — Asplundh's tree trimming dominance: 28 journeyman TT calls out of Newberg/Mollala for wildfire work and routine vegetation, CDL + First Aid/CPR required. 

Data-center buildout is the real engine right now—Grand Forks, El Paso, and Memphis are all chasing Google/fiber/Polaris money, and the work's pushing deeper into book 2 across every region.
